title: Clear 3 standing test failures: route-introspection + leaked Windows cwd (triage bucket A)
intent: Triage of the standing API-test failures (first visible after #166 cleared the radon collection crash) found a batch of pure test-side defects. Fix 3: (A1) two CAM intent-migration parity tests broke on fastapi>=0.137's nested-include_router representation; (A2) a saddle-compensation CLI test hard-codes a Windows absolute cwd that does not exist on Ubuntu CI. Test-only; no production code, no routes, no behavior change. (Triage bucket B — a get_blueprint_adapter ImportError — was reclassified during grounding as a PRODUCTION regression in e2e_spine_runner.py and is NOT in this batch.) NOTE: an earlier revision of this patch tried to fix A1 with a defensive `_route_paths` collector; that only suppressed the original AttributeError and the assertions still failed, because under fastapi>=0.137 nested include_router keeps _IncludedRouter wrappers whose children carry *relative* paths — so an assembled path like /api/cam/drilling/intent-gcode never appears as a single string in app.routes. Root-cause verified: the endpoints are mounted and serve (the live-200/409/422 tests in the same classes pass); only the app.routes path-string introspection could not see them. Corrected approach: assert reachability instead of introspecting the private route tree.

diff_articulation.what_changed: (1) test_drilling_intent_migration.py + test_pocketing_intent_migration.py: replace `{r.path for r in client.app.routes}` route-tree introspection (which under fastapi>=0.137 raised AttributeError on _IncludedRouter wrappers, and — once that was worked around — still asserted-false because nested children carry relative paths) with a `_route_registered(client, path)` helper that probes reachability: `client.post(path, json={}).status_code != 404`. An unmounted path yields 404; a mounted one yields 200/405/409/422. This tests the property the parity check actually cares about (the lane is mounted and not displaced) and is immune to fastapi's internal route-tree representation. The asserted legacy/sibling CAM routes are all confirmed present in code (drilling/pocketing/profiling intent_router POST /intent-gcode, mounted via the CAM aggregator). (2) test_saddle_compensation.py::test_cli_write_example_csv: replace a hard-coded `cwd="C:/Users/thepr/Downloads/luthiers-toolbox/services/api"` (a leaked Windows dev path; FileNotFoundError on Ubuntu CI) with `cwd=str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1])` (the services/api dir relative to the test file); add `from pathlib import Path`.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: These are real standing failures on main (surfaced after #166 fixed the collection crash), not flakes, and both are defects in the TESTS, not the product. Root cause of A1 was verified, not assumed: the drilling/pocketing intent endpoints are mounted and serve — the live 200/409/422 tests in the very same test classes pass on CI; only the app.routes path-string introspection could not reconstruct the assembled path under fastapi>=0.137's nested _IncludedRouter wrappers (relative child paths + path-less wrappers). Reachability probing sidesteps that entirely. A2's leaked Windows cwd can never resolve on a Linux runner. Fixing them shrinks the standing-red baseline with zero product risk and de-taxes future base-comparisons.

commands_run: services/api .venv: pytest tests/test_saddle_compensation.py::TestCsvRoundTrip::test_cli_write_example_csv -> 1 passed (102s; portable cwd works)

root cause verified on PR #169's own CI run (28460040141): live 200/409/422 tests in TestDrillingIntentRouterIntegration / TestPocketingIntentRouterIntegration PASS (endpoints mounted+serving); only the two path-introspection tests FAIL with AssertionError '/api/cam/.../intent-gcode' not in {app.routes paths} -> the assembled path is genuinely absent as a string under fastapi>=0.137 nested include_router, NOT a missing route

code-confirmed all three asserted routes exist: drilling/pocketing/profiling intent_router define POST /intent-gcode, mounted via cam aggregator include_router(prefix=/api/cam -> /drilling|/pocketing|/profiling)
py_compile of all 3 edited test files -> OK

note: the two CAM migration tests use a full-app TestClient (local app import >2 min, and this .venv is fastapi 0.121.1 not the 0.137 CI pin) so they are an Ubuntu-CI witness; the reachability logic (404 iff unmounted) is version-independent

files.intent: Parity check by route reachability (_route_registered: 404 iff unmounted), replacing app.routes path-string introspection that fails under fastapi>=0.137 nested _IncludedRouter wrappers
Portable subprocess cwd (services/api via __file__), replacing a leaked Windows absolute path
